question: Will the highest temperature in Seattle be between 58-59°F on March 23?
description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Seattle-Tacoma International Airport Station in degrees Fahrenheit on 23 Mar '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Seattle-Tacoma International Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/us/wa/seatac/KSEA.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment clusters tightly around 50-53°F highs for Seattle on March 23, driven by the latest NOAA and ECMWF model ensembles projecting daytime maxima in that narrow band amid persistent marine stratus clouds and light southerly winds capping temperatures. The National Weather Service's short-range forecast aligns with a 51-52°F peak under partly cloudy skies, edging out slightly cooler 50-51°F odds due to subtle ensemble spreads where GFS runs trend 1-2°F warmer than cooler Euro analogs. Historical March 23 averages hover near 54°F, but a lingering cool upper-air pattern suppresses potential warming; key uncertainty lies in afternoon cloud breaks, which could nudge outcomes toward 52-53°F if solar insolation dominates late-day. Upcoming 12z model updates will likely sharpen this razor-thin differentiation.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment favors cooler outcomes for New York City's highest temperature on March 23, with 51°F or below leading at 40% implied probability, driven by the latest NOAA Weather Prediction Center and NWS ensemble forecasts converging on highs near 50-53°F amid a stubborn cool northerly flow and marine stratus clouds limiting solar insolation. Urban heat island effects may add 1-2°F to official KATL observations, with peak heat 2-4 PM ET amid low precipitation odds.

Key variables driving outcomes include cloud cover—sparse for 22°C+ (14.5% combined)—reducing solar insolation for 19-20°C probabilities (39%); strengthening southerlies could drop to 18°C or below (9.8%) via advection of cooler air masses. High uncertainty stems from model divergence on frontal timing, with historical March maxima averaging 20.5°C adding baseline context.

This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Munich Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 23 Mar '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Munich Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/de/munich/EDDM.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment for Munich's highest temperature on March 23 hinges on the latest ECMWF and ICON model ensembles, which cluster peaks around 12-13°C amid a mild Atlantic high-pressure ridge, with 13°C edging ahead at 28% implied probability due to subtle diurnal warming in clearer afternoon scenarios. Differentiating factors include variable low-level cloud cover from residual fronts—potentially capping at 12°C (24.5%)—versus sunnier breaks pushing 14°C (15.5%), as seen in recent 00Z runs showing a 1-2°C spread. Historical late-March norms average 11-12°C at Munich Airport station, but current jet stream positioning favors the upper end; watch DWD's next update for resolution shifts.

question: Will the highest temperature in Tel Aviv be 14°C or below on March 23?
description:

The resolution source for this market will be information from NOAA, specifically the highest reading under the "Temp" column on the specified date once information is finalized for all hours on that date, available here: https://www.weather.gov/wrh/timeseries?site=LLBG

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus favors a Tel Aviv high of 20°C (34.5% implied probability) on March 23, driven by ensemble weather model outputs from ECMWF and GFS converging on mild spring conditions with daytime maxima of 19-21°C under partly cloudy skies and light southerly winds. High uncertainty, evident in the broad distribution, arises from model divergences on a potential low-pressure trough over the eastern Mediterranean: strengthening southerlies could push outcomes to 22°C+ (12% combined) via warm air advection, while northerly shifts or increased cloud cover might cap at 17-18°C (22.5% combined). Historical March averages hover at 19.5°C, with urban heat island effects in Tel Aviv amplifying peaks by 1-2°C; key watches include Israel Meteorological Service updates 24 hours out.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors a Tokyo high of 15°C (29.5% implied probability), closely trailed by 16°C (23%) and 14°C (18%), reflecting tight alignment in ensemble weather model forecasts from the Japan Meteorological Agency (JMA) and global systems like ECMWF and GFS. Recent 12Z model runs indicate mild southerly flow and above-average sea surface temperatures off Honshu boosting highs into the mid-teens Celsius, countering a lingering cool air mass from northern fronts. Historical March 23 averages hover at 14.2°C, but urban heat island effects in central Tokyo often add 1-2°C; uncertainty stems from diurnal cloud cover and late-day convection, with JMA's point forecast at 16°C tilting odds warmer while variability differentiates the cluster. Key watch: evening soundings for final convective potential.

This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Tokyo Haneda Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 23 Mar '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Tokyo Haneda Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/jp/tokyo/RJTT.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors a Tokyo high of 15°C (29.5% implied probability), closely trailed by 16°C (23%) and 14°C (18%), reflecting tight alignment in ensemble weather model forecasts from the Japan Meteorological Agency (JMA) and global systems like ECMWF and GFS. Recent 12Z model runs indicate mild southerly flow and above-average sea surface temperatures off Honshu boosting highs into the mid-teens Celsius, countering a lingering cool air mass from northern fronts. Historical March 23 averages hover at 14.2°C, but urban heat island effects in central Tokyo often add 1-2°C; uncertainty stems from diurnal cloud cover and late-day convection, with JMA's point forecast at 16°C tilting odds warmer while variability differentiates the cluster. Key watch: evening soundings for final convective potential.

question: Will the highest temperature in Hong Kong be 18°C or below on March 23?
description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded by the Hong Kong Observatory in degrees Celsius on 23 Mar '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the Hong Kong Observatory, specifically the "Absolute Daily Max (deg. C)" the specified date once information is finalized in the relevant "Daily Extract", available here: https://www.weather.gov.hk/en/cis/climat.htm

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures in Celsius to one decimal place (eg, 9.1°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Tight contest between 27°C (31.5%) and 28°C or higher (29.5%) stems from Hong Kong Observatory's latest forecast indicating a high of 26-28°C on March 23, driven by persistent southerly winds advecting warm, moist air from the South China Sea amid light cloud cover. This edges out 26°C (22%) as traders weigh afternoon convective heating and urban heat island effects boosting peaks above seasonal norms of 24°C. Ensemble models like ECMWF show slight divergence, with 40% chance of exceeding 27.5°C if high pressure stabilizes, while recent days' 25-26°C readings temper aggressive bets; resolution hinges on official HKO measurement at King's Park.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the Hong Kong Observatory, specifically the "Absolute Daily Max (deg. C)" the specified date once information is finalized in the relevant "Daily Extract", available here: https://www.weather.gov.hk/en/cis/climat.htm

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures in Celsius to one decimal place (eg, 9.1°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Tight contest between 27°C (31.5%) and 28°C or higher (29.5%) stems from Hong Kong Observatory's latest forecast indicating a high of 26-28°C on March 23, driven by persistent southerly winds advecting warm, moist air from the South China Sea amid light cloud cover. This edges out 26°C (22%) as traders weigh afternoon convective heating and urban heat island effects boosting peaks above seasonal norms of 24°C. Ensemble models like ECMWF show slight divergence, with 40% chance of exceeding 27.5°C if high pressure stabilizes, while recent days' 25-26°C readings temper aggressive bets; resolution hinges on official HKO measurement at King's Park.

question: Will the highest temperature in Shanghai be 11°C or below on March 23?
description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Shanghai Pudong International Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 23 Mar '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Shanghai Pudong International Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/cn/shanghai/ZSPD.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Shanghai's March 23 high temperature clusters tightly around 15°C (31.5%) and 16°C (29.5%), driven by the latest ECMWF and GFS ensemble forecasts projecting peaks of 15-16°C amid mild southerly flows and partial cloud cover. Differentiating factors include model spread: ECMWF leans slightly cooler at 15°C due to anticipated mid-afternoon stratus, while GFS edges warmer toward 16°C with stronger solar insolation. Historical March 23 norms average 13°C, but this year's El Niño aftermath boosts odds for the upper teens versus cooler 14°C (10%). Upcoming 12Z model runs and Shanghai observatory data could shift probabilities, as urban heat island effects amplify 0.5-1°C in observations.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Shanghai Pudong International Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/cn/shanghai/ZSPD.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Shanghai's March 23 high temperature clusters tightly around 15°C (31.5%) and 16°C (29.5%), driven by the latest ECMWF and GFS ensemble forecasts projecting peaks of 15-16°C amid mild southerly flows and partial cloud cover. Differentiating factors include model spread: ECMWF leans slightly cooler at 15°C due to anticipated mid-afternoon stratus, while GFS edges warmer toward 16°C with stronger solar insolation. Historical March 23 norms average 13°C, but this year's El Niño aftermath boosts odds for the upper teens versus cooler 14°C (10%). Upcoming 12Z model runs and Shanghai observatory data could shift probabilities, as urban heat island effects amplify 0.5-1°C in observations.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket tilts toward 33°C (35%) as Singapore's highest temperature on March 23, driven by the latest ECMWF and GFS ensemble forecasts from NEA and international models projecting peak afternoon heat around 33°C amid reduced cloud cover and lingering El Niño influences. Differentiating factors include potential sea breeze moderation capping at 32°C (22.5%) if thundery showers develop early, versus 34°C (26.5%) under prolonged sunshine amplifying urban heat island effects in a city-state averaging 32.5°C March highs historically. Baseline humidity near 80% tempers extremes, but minimal overnight cooling—recent nights at 27°C—fuels daytime buildup, with resolution hinging on NEA's hourly observations at Changi.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket tilts toward 33°C (35%) as Singapore's highest temperature on March 23, driven by the latest ECMWF and GFS ensemble forecasts from NEA and international models projecting peak afternoon heat around 33°C amid reduced cloud cover and lingering El Niño influences. Differentiating factors include potential sea breeze moderation capping at 32°C (22.5%) if thundery showers develop early, versus 34°C (26.5%) under prolonged sunshine amplifying urban heat island effects in a city-state averaging 32.5°C March highs historically. Baseline humidity near 80% tempers extremes, but minimal overnight cooling—recent nights at 27°C—fuels daytime buildup, with resolution hinging on NEA's hourly observations at Changi.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus tilts toward 15°C as Warsaw's highest temperature on March 23, driven by the latest ECMWF and GFS ensemble forecasts projecting daytime highs in the 14-16°C range under a mild westerly flow from the Atlantic. eventMetadata.context_description: Latest short-range forecasts from Taiwan's Central Weather Administration and global models like ECMWF and GFS drive the tight race between 27°C (36.5%) and 26°C (27%) for Taipei's March 23 high, projecting a peak near 26.5°C under mild southerly flows post-cold front. Differentiating factors include variable afternoon cloud cover suppressing peaks below 27°C in shadier ensemble members, versus urban heat island amplification and light winds allowing 27°C in clearer runs; diurnal heating in Taipei's basin adds 2-3°C above rural baselines. Historical late-March highs average 24-25°C, but recent warm anomalies tilt odds upward, with resolution hinging on exact 1400-1600h observations amid 1-2°C model spread.
